Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

  ## Character
  
  Cycle-driven debug. Each turn does exactly one cycle — execute the next reachable step, surface what fails, diagnose root cause, recommend fix shape, then stop. No auto-fix. The stop keeps the operator in the loop.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Step through reachable RELEASE.md-equivalent today: Payor health probe → pristine-lifecycle cases run one-by-one via `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh <case>`.
  - Re-run marshal-zero ONLY when a case demands fresh blank state. Case 1 (`rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation`) is the only such case — cases 2/3 self-install throwaway prefixes via `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs:565,706`, idempotent on `prlc-`/`prlr-`).
  - After a code-fix lands for a defect surfaced by a case, retry that single case directly; do not bounce through marshal-zero unless the new failure demonstrates blank state is required.
  - Out-of-reach this pace: canonical `rbw-tP` infrastructure, supply chain (inscribe/ordain), Crucible suite, cleanup. Reachable when `rbw-tP` lands in a follow-on pace.
  - Per surfaced issue, route disposition: notch into BBAAM if in-scope-blocking the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope, capture as itch if speculative.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract (waived to main for initial debug per operator).
  - No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis is described.
  - Diagnosed-but-unfixed issues accumulate as scar candidates for wrap-time routing.
  - Multi-officium discipline: dirty files in tree from sibling officia (e.g., BBAAN spec sweep) do NOT block per-case runs; they only gate marshal-zero's tree-clean precondition. Don't conflate.
  - gcloud is for diagnosis only — do not mutate GCP state through it.

f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. Repair (Maximal scope per operator nod): added -A to all four 'openssl enc -base64 -d' decode sites — (1) rbgp_Payor.sh:1355 (governor mantle key decode, case-3 blocking); (2) rbgg_Governor.sh:242 (sibling SA invest decode, would have blocked next cycle on invest paths); (3-4) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:959,1187 (build provenance decodes, latent until conjure/bind/graft exercised — same root fault). Single-flag addition per site; matches project's own encode-side pattern; cross-binary verified. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Five sites total in Tools/rbk/ have 'openssl enc -base64 -d' — the fifth (vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228) is in FUTURE/, not active code, deliberately not touched. f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. 31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. rbgo_get_token_capture() {
  zrbgo_sentinel

  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Exchange service account credentials for OAuth2 access token"
  buc_doc_param "rbra_file" "Path to RBRA credentials file containing RBRA_* variables"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1

  buc_log_args "Build JWT"
  local z_jwt
  z_jwt=$(zrbgo_build_jwt_capture "${z_rbra_file}") || return 1

  buc_log_args "Exchange for OAuth token"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture "${z_jwt}") || return 1

  printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbgg_*)

rbgg_list_service_accounts() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all service accounts in the project"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Listing service accounts in project: '"${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token (rc=$?)"

  buc_log_args 'List service accounts via REST API'
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts"                     "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}"

  local z_count
  z_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}" '.accounts | length') \
    || buc_die "Failed to parse response"

  if test "${z_count}" = "0"; then
    buc_info "No service accounts found in project"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_step "Found ${z_count} service account(s):"

printf '%s' "${z_key_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_key_json}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"

  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
  local -r z_rbra_file="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/governor-${z_timestamp}.rbra"

  local z_client_email
  z_client_email=$(jq -r '.client_email' "${z_key_json}") || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email"
  test -n "${z_client_email}" || buc_die "Empty client_email in key JSON"

  local z_private_key
  z_private_key=$(jq -r '.private_key' "${z_key_json}") || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key"
  test -n "${z_private_key}" || buc_die "Empty private_key in key JSON"

  local z_project_id
  z_project_id=$(jq -r '.project_id' "${z_key_json}") || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id"
  test -n "${z_project_id}" || buc_die "Empty project_id in key JSON"

  buc_step 'Write RBRA file'
  {
    printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n' "${RBCC_role_governor}"
    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "${z_client_email}"
    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "${z_private_key}"; printf '"\n'
    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "${z_project_id}"
    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=1800\n'
  } > "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"

  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"

  rm -f "${z_key_json}" || buc_die "Failed to remove temp key file: ${z_key_json}"

  buc_success "Governor reset completed successfully"
  buc_info "Governor service account: ${z_governor_email}"
  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_rbra_file}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Install the RBRA file:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${z_rbra_file} ${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Or, for assay (test) mode:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${z_rbra_file} ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
}

# eof

content.text:
Stopping per cycle discipline. Diagnosis follows.

## Cycle 5 — diagnosis (no fix lands without nod)

**Cycle 4 verified:** Mantle now completes all 12 steps cleanly. Decode no longer silently produces 0 bytes; RBRA assembly succeeded; client_email, private_key, project_id all extracted; RBRA file written. The `-A` flag fix is good.

**New surface:** Case 3 (`rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle`) advances past mantle, fails at the immediately-following `rbw-aL` (list SAs).

```
rbgo_OAuth.sh:190: Validate RBRA file exists
rbgg_Governor.sh:439: ERROR: Failed to get admin token (rc=1)
```

**Root cause — fixture/mantle integration gap, not a code regression:**

- `rbgp_Payor.sh:1359` writes RBRA to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/governor-${ts}.rbra` — a per-invoke transient location.
- `rbgp_Payor.sh:1391-1394` prints two `cp` instructions (governor or assay rbra.env) for the **operator** to manually install.
- `rbgu_Utility.sh:215-218` (governor token resolver) reads `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` which derives to `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/governor/rbra.env` (= `../station-files/secrets/governor/rbra.env`).
- Fixture `rbtdrp_pristine.rs:760-800` invokes `mantle` (invoke-00002) then `list-present` (invoke-00003) back-to-back. Each invoke gets its own per-invoke `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`, so the RBRA from invoke-00002 is invisible to invoke-00003. Fixture has **no install step** between them.
- `../station-files/secrets/governor/` is empty (verified). `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` does not exist; auth fails.

This was always broken; pre-Cycle-4 it never got reached because mantle died one step earlier on the silent-decode bug. Cycle 4 unblocked it; this is the immediately-next defect.

**Fix shape — recommendation:**

**Shape A — Fixture-side install (preferred, in-scope-blocking):**
1. Mantle emits a new fact `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_RBRA_PATH` carrying `${z_rbra_file}` — single-line addition next to the existing `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL` emission at `rbgp_Payor.sh:1303`.
2. Fixture reads the fact via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` (matches existing SA-email consumption), then `std::fs::copy` into `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (resolved via the same secrets_dir read pattern already in `rbtdrp_check_rbra_files`).
3. Teardown: governor_lifecycle's existing `forfeit` step deletes the SA but not the installed RBRA. Add `std::fs::remove_file(rbra_dest)` after forfeit so case ends in pristine state (preserving marshal-zero invariant for downstream cases).

**Shape B — Mantle auto-installs to `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`** (structural, behavior change to operator semantics). Per docket: structural → slate as separate pace. Not recommended for this cycle.

**Live GCP state update:**
- `prlc-d-pristg100000` ACTIVE (Cycle 3 leftover, decode failure)
- `prlc-d-pristg100001` ACTIVE (this cycle's levy + mantle success; SA `governor-202604271318` present)
- Fixture's next pristg pick will be `100002`.

Recommendation: apply Shape A. Awaiting nod.

content.text:
Good probe. Short answer: **the assay path is exactly the right slot semantically, but it's not load-bearing yet — no consumer reads from it.** Detail:

**What "assay" is today** (per `BBAAB df8f1ce4` "Pristine-lifecycle foundation"):
- Constants exist: `RBCC_role_assay="assay"`, `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/assay/rbra.env"`.
- Marshal-zero zeros the assay/ dir alongside governor/director/retriever (`rblm_cli.sh:80,149`).
- Producer-side cp **suggestions** print in mantle/invest output (`rbgp_Payor.sh:1394`, `rbgg_Governor.sh:520,675`).
- **Spec mandates** producer-output discipline — director_invest/retriever_invest **write RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively** (`RBS0:1067, 1105`), with `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` writes scheduled to retire. Mantle is silent in spec on this but symmetry implies the same.

**What's missing — the consumer side:**
- `rbgu_get_governor_token_capture` (`rbgu_Utility.sh:215-218`) hardcodes `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`.
- Zero references to `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` in `rbgu_Utility.sh` / `rbgo_OAuth.sh`. The slot is never read.

So "copy to assay" alone doesn't unblock case 3 — list-present still looks at governor's slot and fails.

**Two shapes to make assay load-bearing for case 3:**

**Shape C — assay-first lookup chain (cleanest, completes BBAAB):**
- `rbgu_get_governor_token_capture` tries `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` first, falls through to `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`. Symmetric for director/retriever resolvers.
- Mantle/invest retire the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` write per spec — write directly to `${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}` (clobbers OK; marshal-zero invariant means it's empty pre-mantle, and the assay slot's whole purpose is throwaway test creds).
- Fixture: no install step needed — auth resolver finds it in assay automatically. Cleanup is `std::fs::remove_file(RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE)` after forfeit.
- Operator-facing cp instructions to GOVERNOR slot remain valid for production install (operator chooses when to promote).

**Shape D — fixture cp's into governor slot directly (mechanical, narrowest):**
- Fixture copies `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/governor-*.rbra` → `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`. Cleanup at end. This is what I sketched as Shape A earlier with new fact emission — but no fact needed since the file naming `governor-*.rbra` is glob-stable.
- Doesn't pollute assay/ but does pollute the production governor slot for the case duration; relies on case cleanup. Doesn't advance the spec's stated retirement intent.

**Recommendation:** Shape C. It's the spec-direction work, it makes the assay slot actually load-bearing instead of aspirational, and it's a small directed change — three resolver touch-ups (governor/director/retriever, fall-through pattern) plus retiring three producer-side BURD_OUTPUT_DIR writes (governor mantle, director invest, retriever invest) to write to assay directly.

The "safety" you're naming is real — assay is the designated throwaway slot, and using it keeps test/debug runs out of the production credential paths.

One caveat for scope: Shape C touches sibling director/retriever invest sites symmetrically (since their spec text already mandates the producer-side change). That's a larger blast radius than Cycle 4's `-A` flag sweep but parallel in shape — universal application of an already-documented pattern. Alternative: scope this cycle to mantle + governor resolver only, leave director/retriever invest for a follow-up. Either way works; the symmetric sweep is what the spec is calling for.

Awaiting nod on shape and scope.

content.text:
## Character

The load-bearing pace. Establishes the pristine-lifecycle fixture infrastructure and lands case 1 (marshal-zero-attestation gate). Foundation work already committed in df8f1ce4. Cases 2-5 split to sibling paces for parallel execution.

## Work

### Foundation (already committed in df8f1ce4)

- `RBCC_role_assay="assay"` constant in rbcc_Constants.sh
- `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` derived path + assay/ subdirectory mkdir in rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh
- `rblm_zero` pre-checks: working tree clean + HEAD pushed (fail-fast before buc_require)
- `rblm_zero` cleanup includes assay/rbra.env
- `rblm_zero` auto-commits at end with descriptive message (no signature trailer)
- SA-creator success messages display assay path option alongside canonical

### Remaining

1. Mint pristine-lifecycle fixture skeleton in rbtdrm_manifest.rs and rbtdrc_crucible.rs
2. Add per-fixture fail_fast (rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast paralleling rbtdrc_needs_charge); plumb through main.rs run_suite to abort full-fixture mode if case 1 fails
3. Implement case 1 (marshal-zero-attestation): asserts (a) working tree clean, (b) RBRR fields blank — 5 site-specific fields rblm_zero blanks, (c) no RBRA credential files at governor/director/retriever/assay paths, (d) no hallmark fields in nameplates, (e) no depot-scoped vessel fields. Failure surfaces ALL violations in one diagnostic.
4. Wire tabtargets: tt/rbtd-r.Run.pristine-lifecycle.sh, tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh

## Verification

- Run full fixture against accumulated state: case 1 fails with complete diagnostic listing all 5 violation classes
- Run tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh, then full fixture: case 1 passes
- Single-case marshal-zero-attestation: same outcomes
- Bash syntax + theurge cargo build clean

## Out of scope

- Cases 2-5 (sibling paces for cases 2-3 and 4-5)
- rbw-tP wrapper (later pace)
- Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC)

d2dec554 jjb:1011-43463b1e:₢BBAAM:n: Step 5 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: pristine-lifecycle Rust fixture (cases 2 & 3) reshape to consume Step 4's per-moniker fact-file infrastructure. Closes the depot-identity-collapse loop — producer (rbgp_depot_list emitting <moniker>.depot files) is now matched by a Rust consumer (theurge picking the next free moniker per family stem). (1) Constants: dropped static moniker consts RBTDRP_DEPOT_MONIKER_LIFECYCLE='pristq01' and RBTDRP_DEPOT_MONIKER_GOVERNOR='pristg01' (replaced by family stems + autodetect), dropped RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID (orphan after Step 2 dropped the producer); added RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_LIFECYCLE='pristq', RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_GOVERNOR='pristg', RBTDRP_FAMILY_NUMERIC_FLOOR: u32 = 100000, RBTDRP_FAMILY_NUMERIC_WIDTH: usize = 6, RBTDRP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT='depot' (mirror of bash RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT). (2) New helper rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker(list_result, family_stem) walks the depot_list invocation's BURV output dir for <family>NNNNNN.depot files, strips suffix and prefix, requires exact six-digit numeric width (so legacy two-digit names like 'pristq01' can't be parsed as 1 and shadow real entries), parses to u32, returns <family><max+1>. Returns <family>FLOOR when no matching files exist or the output dir is missing (first-run case). (3) New helper rbtdrp_compose_project_id(root, moniker) reads RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX from rbrr.env and returns ${cloud_prefix}d-${moniker} — mirrors RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation, used post-levy purely for the diagnostic project-id.txt tee since the fact-file producer is gone. (4) Case 2 (rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle_impl) flow reshaped: install_throwaway_prefixes → invoke depot_list 'list-pre' (probe existing depots, populate fact files) → rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker(list_pre, FAMILY_STEM_LIFECYCLE) → rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker(picked) → invoke depot_levy zero-arg → rbtdrp_compose_project_id for tee → invoke depot_list 'list-present' + assert project_id in stdout → invoke depot_unmake zero-arg → invoke depot_list 'list-after' + assert absent-or-DELETE_REQUESTED. Doc comment updated to describe the autodetect+zero-arg lifecycle. (5) Case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle_impl) flow reshaped with same prelude (probe → pick FAMILY_STEM_GOVERNOR → install → levy → compose); removed the buggy STEP-5-PENDING block that previously passed project_id as the moniker (Step-1 artifact — the install_depot_moniker(project_id) call was semantically inverted, installing the full project_id string into RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER which would have fatal'd zrbrr_enforce post-Step-2 once the regex check '^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$' rejected the embedded hyphen). Mantle/forfeit/list assertions unchanged (governor SA email is the unit of identity for that segment). Cleanup unmake call switched to zero-arg. (6) STEP-5-PENDING annotation removed. Build green: rbtd-b. Tests not yet run — case 1 (marshal-zero attestation) is fast-suite and will pass; cases 2-3 are crucible/service tier and require live GCP credentials. The pristine-lifecycle fixture as a whole is now reachable post-collapse but real exercise still needs service-tier credentials. RCG file-size threshold: file grew from 760 to 885 lines (over 800-line stop-and-ask threshold); not refactored as part of this notch — user direction pending on whether to split case-1 attestation off into its own file in a follow-on pace, or leave as is. With Step 5 landed, BBAAM's seven paddock locks all have their producers + consumers + fixtures aligned; pace ready to wrap on user signal.
3c9d38fb jjb:1011-94a014eb:₢BBAAM:n: Step 1 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: kindle infrastructure plus mechanical sweep. RBDC adds RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID (${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}), RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar), RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool), and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET (${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}); type markers and pool/tether/airgap suffixes are literal strings inside RBDC to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering (existing furnishers run rbdc-then-rbgc in most modules, rbgc-then-rbdc in others — uniformity left for a future cleanup). RBGC drops obsolete RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX and RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX (project_id and bucket are now installation-prefix-anchored, not timestamp-suffixed) and gains depot-state vocabulary tinder constants (RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED) plus RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT for the per-moniker fact-file shape coming in Step 4. RBGD switches RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID throughout, drops the prefix-strip-and-timestamp depot-name extraction logic (RBGD_DEPOT_NAME is now just RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER), and reshapes RBGD_GCS_BUCKET via RBDC_GCS_BUCKET. Marshal-zero (rblm_cli.sh) banner copy and sed cases adjusted to the new field set. Compose bridge: rbob_bottle.sh writes a temp env file (rbob_rbdc_compose.env) at zrbob_kindle with RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, added to the docker compose --env-file list; .rbk/rbob_compose.yml image refs switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY to the RBDC equivalents. Operator's .rbk/rbrr.env reshaped to the new field set with RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=depot10041 placeholder. Mechanical RBRR_X -> RBDC_X sweep across all consumers: rbgg_Governor, rbgv_AccessProbe, rbgp_Payor (depot-list regex sites replaced with string-prefix match against ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-, post-levy 'Update RBRR configuration' banner replaced with informational 'Depot resources created' summary, governor-mantle docstring and error message rewritten around the new derivation), rbgp_cli (differential-furnish comment annotated Step-2-pending), rbgb_Buckets, rbga_ArtifactRegistry, rbob_bottle (GAR ref via RBDC), rbrn_cli, rbfv_FoundryVerify, rbfc_FoundryCore, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild, rbfl_FoundryLedger, rbhpq_quota_build (also RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM -> RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM), rbhpb_base. Onboarding probes that file-scan rbrr.env to detect 'depot configured' switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER: rbhocc_crash_course, rbhodf_director_first_build, rbhoda_director_airgap, rbhodb_director_bind, rbhodg_director_graft. Theurge minimal-touch: rbtdrp_pristine.rs RBTDRP_RBRR_BLANK_FIELDS shrinks to {CLOUD_PREFIX, RUNTIME_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER}; helper rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id renamed to rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker (case 3 call site updated, but the ordering is service-tier-broken until Step 5 reshapes case 3). rbtdrf_fast.rs negative tests rbrr-missing-project-id -> rbrr-missing-moniker (unset RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) and rbrr-bad-pool-stem -> rbrr-bad-moniker (export RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=BAD-MONIKER triggers format check). Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58 unit tests), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97 across enrollment-validation/regime-validation/regime-smoke/handbook-render), vow-b. Step 2 (rbgp_depot_levy reshape — drop timestamp, drop CLI arg, use RBDC, drop post-levy banner) is the next pace; rbgp_depot_levy in this state is left functionally intact with its old CLI-arg flow but its post-creation values can no longer be 'pasted into RBRR' (they're derived now), so the function is effectively unusable until Step 2 lands.

Backs out the four-file Rust/bash/spec change so the pace can be re-scoped via paddock and docket health work. Close the zrbrr_enforce gap surfaced in BBAAJ — credentialed cases hitting any non-rbgp_depot_levy command would fail at differential-furnish enforcement because RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM are blanked by marshal-zero. Field-name consts RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY/GCB_POOL_STEM mirror the existing RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID pattern; the blank-fields array now references all three by const rather than the prior two literals. Per-case diagnostic tee filenames lifted to a small RBTDRP_TEE_* const block (RCG Constant Discipline — Identity Rule: each const names its specific value, 2+ occurrence threshold met by case 2/3 duplication of project-id.txt and sa-email.txt; new gar-repository.txt/gcb-pool-stem.txt also const-extracted at first-touch). Build green, unit tests 58/58 pass (existing rbtdtp_pristine.rs tests didn't reference the renamed helper, so no test surface invalidation), fast suite 97/97 pass across 4 fixtures. Step 1 stands alone as the foundational fix-up; cases 4 and 5 will land in subsequent notches.
e59c49b8 jjb:1011-e5f05d8e:₢BBAAJ:n: Pristine-lifecycle cases 2 (depot) and 3 (governor) land with fact-file invocation pattern. RBS0 spec gains a Payor Fact Files section with both new linked terms; manifest tests refactored — pristine-lifecycle dropped from the empty-manifest fast-tier test, two new tests added for the populated arm and missing-colophon error path.

- **BBAAB** ("phase-0-pristine-preflight-detector") is **closed** — it landed only the assay constants + marshal-zero attestation (case 1). It's behind us, not ahead.

Character is mechanical — the new shape is settled in the paddock; this pace adapts theurge to it. ## Scope

Pristine-lifecycle section restructures. BBAAJ's §2 (pristine-lifecycle-simple) and BBAAK's original §3 (pristine-lifecycle-credentialed) collapse into a single unified section containing three fused cases that share one throwaway depot for the run:

- Depot stand-up — depot levy and the rbrr field install needed to satisfy downstream preconditions.
- SA cycle — governor mantle, retriever invest, director invest, access-probes, divests of operational SAs.
- Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup.

Test consumes per-row observation facts via type-extension lookup. All stdout-substring assertions retire from pristine-lifecycle code. Test-side primitives reduce to tabtarget invocation, file copy (assay → role production path), `Path::exists()` on facts, and one fact-read for depot project_id.

Manifest registration: add new cult-verb colophons; remove the colophons retired by BBAAN.

Cost reduction: one throwaway depot per fixture run, down from four under the original BBAAJ+BBAAK design.

## BBAAN sequencing implications

These flow from BBAAN's settled implementation conventions and constrain the fused-case ordering.

**Post-divest production RBRA absence.** "Divests of operational SAs" leaves no production RBRA at the role's path (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA decision). Any subsequent fixture step that needs those credentials must re-invest first. The current SA-cycle ordering (mantle → invest → access-probes → divest) is correct as written; tear-down does not need credentials.

**Invest fatal-on-exists.** Re-invest within a single fixture run requires divest first (BBAAN's fatal-if-exists decision). The fused-case design already lands invest exactly once per role; no in-run re-invest needed.

**Governor cleanup folded into unmake.** No separate "forfeit governor" step appears in the fused case — `Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup` is the entire cleanup. The 404-tolerant unmake makes ordering robust to mid-fixture failures (an unmake after a failed mantle still cleans up cleanly).

**RBCC fact-extension constants.** BBAAN consolidates fact-extension tinder constants into one contiguous block in RBCC (migrating `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC; new retriever/director ext constants join it). Rust-side consumers — including `RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and any new fact-name imports — must point to the RBCC home. Pristine fixture is currently service-tier-broken pending BBAAM Step 5; this remap is part of the bring-back-online work for this pace.

## Constraints

- Depends on BBAAN landing first.
- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract: stop on first failure cleanly; commits during the run are first-class; no recovery scaffolding.
- Reuse paddock-locked patterns; do not re-engineer them here.
- Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude.

## Out of scope

- SA-management surface refactor (BBAAN).
- Phase 1+ of rbw-tP (BBAAC and onward).
- Multi-instance test scenarios beyond single-instance per role.
- Case 1 (marshal-zero gate) — stays in §1, untouched by this rebuild.

## Character

Operator-handbook sweep for the cult-verb refactor (BBAAN) and the producer-output discipline. Lands after BBAAN so handbook narrative describes settled vocabulary, not intent. Designed to run in parallel with downstream coding paces — the handbook surface is operator-facing prose, not depot runtime code, so it doesn't block any depot/foundry work.

Different cognitive posture from the coding paces it reflects — prose with intentional ceremony tone. Best held in a separate session from the coding officium, parallel to BBAAQ. Soft pairing with BBAAQ for vocabulary consistency between spec and operator narrative.

## Scope

Five handbook files in `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`, all with paddock-settled vocabulary by the time this pace mounts.

**rbhogw_governor_wrapper.sh — primary surface.** The canonical operator narrative for SA management.
- Cult-verb constant refs: `RBYC_CHARTER`, `RBYC_KNIGHT`, `RBZ_CHARTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_KNIGHT_DIRECTOR`, `RBZ_LIST_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS` — adapt to whatever BBAAN settles.
- Verb-as-noun ceremony lines (L63, L66, L73): "Create a Retriever with read access (Charter):", "Create a Director with build access (Knight):", "List issued service accounts:" — adjust to the new vocabulary.
- Producer-output narrative (L49-52): if BBAAN's assay-slot-exclusive credential-write changes the operator's RBRA install path, update the install-step instructions accordingly.

**rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh, rbhocd_credential_director.sh — credential install.** Light verb touch-ups in narrative; potential producer-output flow adjustment via the shared `zrbho_credential_install` helper. `RBYC_TALLY` reference in rbhocr (L42) — adjust if tally vocabulary moves.

**rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh — payor wrapper.** `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` reference (L95) — confirm mantle stays unchanged per BBAAN's cult-verb decision (mantle stays as singleton-per-depot exception). Producer-output narrative for governor RBRA placement parallels rbhogw.

**rbhob_base.sh — shared helpers.** `zrbho_credential_install` may need adjustment if BBAAN settles assay-slot-exclusive write semantics — base helper changes ripple consistently across all credential install narratives.

## Operator-visible behaviors from BBAAN

These behaviors must surface in narrative; the handbook is the operator's contract.

**Divest removes the production RBRA.** After successful cloud DELETE, the role's local production RBRA file is also removed. Operator narrative should state this explicitly so the disappearing file isn't a surprise.

**Invest is fatal if the SA already exists.** Re-keying flow is divest-then-invest. Narrative should guide the operator to divest first when re-investing.

**Unmake silently absorbs governor cleanup.** No separate governor forfeit step in the procedure. Operator behavior is unchanged ("just run unmake"); the absence-of-mantle case is invisible to the operator (404-tolerant under the hood).

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Handbook narrative describes what landed in BBAAN — read the actual code state, do not work from intent. If implementation differs from paddock-settled vocabulary, raise it; do not silently reconcile.
- Preserve the existing operator-narrative tone — ceremony language is intentional. Verb swaps should keep the same cadence (e.g., the parenthetical "(Charter):" pattern stays as "(Invest):", not flattened into prose).
- Use existing `buh_line` / `buh_step` / `buh_section` / `buh_tt` primitives — no narrative-structure rework.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices.

## Out of scope

- Code logic changes beyond what's required to consume renamed RBZ/RBYC constants. Structural changes for new producer-output discipline land in BBAAN; this pace adapts narrative to whatever BBAAN settled.
- Handbook files outside the five listed — depot-identity narrative is already swept by BBAAM Step 1; non-SA handbooks (crucible, windows, tadmor, first-crucible, dispatch CLI) don't touch the changing surfaces.
- Spec/README/RBYC vocabulary documentation — owned by sibling pace ₢BBAAQ.
- Restructuring handbook section ordering or step decomposition — out of scope for a vocabulary sweep.
- New onboarding content — out of scope.

## Character

Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
`axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
`axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.

This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
of project history.

Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
each behavioral-contract adaptation.

Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
fields.

## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)

The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
(ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
fresh).

| Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
|---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
| RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
| RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
| RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |

Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
documents the choice.

Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
- D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
- Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits

## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:
